violations.text: The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026
The floors under the storage racks in the dry storage room was observed with old rodent droppings. The physical facilities must be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings in the dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to contact pest control for a full evaluation of the premises (finding entry points, locating holes, putting down more traps/bait stations). Pest control recommended to increase from 1x monthly to 2x monthly or more pending pest control evaluation. All pest control reports to be sent to inspector until rodent activity is resolved. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025
Bags of pasta was observed to be gnawed at in the dry storage area. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

The shelving and floors in the dry storage area was observed with old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

At the time of re-inspection, several food items kept in refrigeration since previous day were observed held between 41 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit or above were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Food items recently supplied to the facility and observed at 44 degrees Fahrenheit or below were moved to colder refrigeration. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

The following equipment in the kitchen area were observed to be in poor repair... - The 2 Door Reach-In Refrigerator was observed to be in poor repair. Upon arrival for re-inspection, the unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures ranging from 41 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Use of the unit for cold holding of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until serviced/repaired/replaced. - The Single Door Cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, items observed in the single door reach-in cooler were recently stocked for lunch service. Use of the unit for cold holding of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until serviced/repaired/replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

The 2 Door Reach-In Cooler located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. At the time of inspection, the 2 door cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, the unit was checked upon arrival for work and observed at 40 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Unit to be monitored for Cold Holding. Per the PIC, work order for the 2 door cooler is to be placed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
